mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold crossing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and stable consumer price inflation indicate a supply-side recovery without demand-pull overheating, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth reflects expanding aggregate demand that directly lifts corporate revenues and earnings per share, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP acceleration correlates negatively with 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 40% of observations, or the rolling 3-year Spearman correlation drops below -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month consumer price index increase exceeding 0.5 percent triggers inflation risk repricing, forcing higher discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered inflation spikes, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 45% across two consecutive rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A continuous month-over-month expansion in the industrial production index signals robust manufacturing and utility output, which directly supports industrial and financial sector earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over a 5-year walk-forward period, or the 20-day forward return median is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with declining housing starts indicate a labor market peak followed by demand contraction, which signals an impending earnings recession and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the cross-sectional predictive power (Rank IC) remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in retail and food services sales reflects weakening consumer demand, which directly reduces corporate top-line growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in over 55% of triggered sales decline periods, or the rolling 1-year correlation between RSAFS changes and 5-day SPX returns exceeds 0.10.
